author: SeasonalAnarchy
title: Useless app and Hopeless support
content:
It used to be a decent player despite its clunky interface. But over time, it has transformed into one of the most frustrating and useless music apps on the entire App Store.

It seems to have a mind of its own — not in an AI way, but in how it randomly decides how long a song should play. And for some reason, it absolutely hates lock screens. The moment your screen locks, it assumes you no longer want to listen to music, stops playback, and even clears the “Now Playing” history — just to make sure you can’t easily resume the track.

As for network and cloud connectivity? It simply doesn’t work. At all.

Customer support is a complete joke. Don’t expect a reply — you’ll never hear back from them.

Which leaves one simple question: what do you call a music player that can’t actually play music?

title: Look elsewhere
content:
Scanning albums on a NAS just doesn’t work in the real world - it only works if you scan ~5 albums at a time.  Any screen lock activity cancels the scan.  Changing to a different screen in the same app cancels the scan.  The developer’s solution is to use the desktop app.  Which doesn’t exist on Windows.
It seems odd that there just doesn’t seem to be any consideration of the user experience.

title: 20 or more frequency band equalizer up to 20,000hz pls?🥲🫶
content: 2nd review of this amazing app the only app on apple store that we can truely rely on to play lossless audio, not perfect but i think in time theyll be fixed crashes a lot i noticed when handling big lossless files but for lossy its smooth, it would be amzing if we can have 20 or more frequency band equalizer on the app🥲🫶 a little fun equalizer for iphone16promax speaker: set the eq to: 32=-12 64=-9 125=2.6 250=-4 500=11 1k=4 2k=-7 4k=-6 8k=-11 16k=11, (i removed the microgrill on the speaker to let the higher frequecy and 250hz go out better), then play lossless audio or lossless/lossy dolby atmos songs on the speaker,if you know where to get it, and viola you now have a handheld lossless/dolby atmos virtual in your hand!! play music and blow people away that sound is coming from an iphone😂, (this could alsowork for 17,15,14promax) note: volume will decrease a little bit but it will be flat or use the db+ if its not loud enoud. Muah

author: Amis2021
title: Excellent app for private music library
content:
Old fashioned idea now days perhaps, but I believe in OWNING my own music. I have been looking for a good app to play and organise my music. This does it all and well in my opinion. Love the option to select higher sample rates for the higher quality tracks. Equaliser is great with a range of presets. Plenty of customisation of screen layout. Love the customisable Audio Player — select an album switch to audio player and easily access the controls required. 

I’ve been using it for a month now every day and it has been working seamlessly on my iPad. 

One complaint: I can’t create nested playlists. I would love to be able to create categories: Christmas, jazz, pop, etc.

author: alissa914
title: Supports a lot of formats but never seems to offline download anything from Dropbox or SMB
content:
Well it seems to work albeit a bit slowly. I couldn’t get it to work with Dropbox though. When it syncs, it seems to work but it takes a long time. I mean…. Any sync action takes forever but I’m starting to think this is more an issue with iOS just not allowing it to run in the background to do all this. But even so, just to load a directory from the cloud took hours and accomplished very little. So I just got a flash drive, put all my music from Dropbox on it, and used Files to put it all on my phone. Works well now. So it’s a great player but don’t buy it for the cloud sync. It doesn’t seem worth it for that but most blame likely goes to iOS there.

Edit 2:  After having it play music for a few days and setting up the Dropbox sync, it synchronized everything. Guess it was the lack of background time it would get. But after it fully synched up, I used it in my CarPlay and it just sounds incredible. Really does. Only gripe is that I can’t find volume leveling but if it’s there, I will find it. If not, I’d highly request that it gets added. Sound quality makes me no longer want iTunes on this phone or Apple Music. This sounds so much better. 

Edit:  seems I can see one problem and it’s that the app can’t be set to run in the background unless it’s actively playing music. iOS seems to put it to sleep so background tasks appear to pause even if plugged in.  Coming from Windows Phone OS, we used to see this a lot there when msft would let its apps do maintenance tasks but no one else could.  I’m guessing it’s a power management thing.  Like here, I copied all the files to my phone directly and have it doing metadata reads. It pauses unless you’re on that settings screen and it’s playing music. Again, seems more like an OS thing which I hope the dev can get permission to allow it to run unpaused in the background as a dev option, but it seems not to be the app problem otherwise. Just a consequence of being limited by Apple

author: Josh.yu
title: Constant Crashes After Subscription Purchase
content: Flacbox used to work well for my setup, but after buying the annual subscription and listening a few times, it now crashes constantly. I’m playing songs stored on my Synology NAS with a DAC attached to my iPhone, and the app often quits after a short period of playback. This makes it very hard to enjoy music and reliably use the app with my existing library and hardware. I hope the developer can fix these stability issues quickly so the app becomes usable again.

author: MaxPowerrrr
title: Amazing offline music player
content: I have been using cloud music solutions for a long time now. While they are great for discovery of new music, I was missing the ability to manage my favorite album collections in an effective way, especially in combination with offline listening. Now with Flacbox, I have total freedom again which of my favorite digitized CDs I would like to have in my collection. Browsing through this collection in Flacbox is super easy, with many options that you would expect to have. And it has great an Apple CarPlay interface, much more effective in finding my favorite albums before starting my drive somewhere than the cloud applications. Flacbox is like having my iPod classic back again, for endless offline listening sessions.

author: Alcydenikopol
title: Excellent
content:
Je suis vraiment très satisfait de cette application. Je l’ai connectée à mon serveur Nextcloud pour lire en streaming toute ma musique stockée localement, et elle permet en plus de diffuser en AirPlay.
L’interface est excellente, fluide, et nettement plus agréable que celle d’Audirvāna — pourtant bien plus cher. Ça fait du bien d’avoir enfin une bonne ergonomie !

Seul petit souci pour le moment : l’application a du mal à synchroniser les dossiers qui contiennent beaucoup de sous-dossiers. Pour contourner ça, j’ai regroupé tous les morceaux de mes grosses playlists dans un seul dossier, et tout fonctionne parfaitement.

title: ‼️Crashes, Developer Unresponsive, Requesting a Refund 💸
content:
I liked this app—when it actually worked. All of a sudden it just quits after a couple of seconds. And I see I’m not the only one having this problem check the other reviews Reddit and Twitter as well. 

I tried all the usual methods to resolve a problem like this. Hard quitting the app, restarting the phone, deleting, and re-downloading the app and NOTHING WORKS. 

Because I hate ridiculous and negative reviews from lazy people, I first tried to contact the developer to resolve the issue. I reached out via email and got no response. I messaged the developer on Reddit and got no response. And then I tried DM the developer on Twitter and got no response. So this is my only option—to leave a one star review that should be a zero star review. 

If the developer pays attention to these reviews at all: I want to request a refund. The app literally does not work.

author: AncientMystic
title: Forcing users to allow data collection unless you pay
content: This app forces users to allow data collection in new versions and treats disabling it as a premium feature. So you have to pay to not submit your data to google, which is borderline illegal under GDPR. Uninstalled. “Freely Given Consent (Article 7(4) and Recital 43) The core issue lies in Article 7(4) of the GDPR, which states: "When assessing whether consent is freely given, utmost account shall be taken of whether... the performance of a contract is made conditional on consent to processing personal data that is not necessary for the performance of that contract." This is reinforced by Recital 43, which clarifies that consent is not freely given if the user has no genuine or free choice or is unable to refuse or withdraw consent without detriment”

author: SalmanZoldyck
title: Great App & Feedback
content: This is really a great app to have, especially since it’s free and offers features that usually only come with paid versions. With some improvements, I think it could be even better. For example, adding a crossfade between songs would be nice, as well as a speed controller (0.5x, 1x, 1.5x, 2x) that actually changes the way the song sounds — like 0.5x giving a slowed & reverbed effect, and 2x making it fast and high-pitched. There used to be an app that did this, called Music Cloud, but it no longer exists. Since then, I’ve tried countless other apps to find the same features, and this is the only one that offers even more. Anyway, those are my thoughts on improvements that I hope will be added.

I’m looking for another basic MP3 player now.

author: dnuken
title: Was für eine geniale App
content: Wer noch eine größere Musiksammlung, beispielsweise mit eigener Ordnerstruktur lokal oder im Netzwerk liegen hat, kann hier wie gewohnt und nicht nach Apples Vorgabesystem Musik abspielen, so wie es sein soll. Für mich ein großer Gewinn um Musik nach meiner Struktur streamen oder abspielen zu können. Ein Download auf Tablet und Handy von der NAS ist ebenfalls möglich. Leider läuft die App nicht mit allen Updates auch auf dem Mac. Eine ältere Version konnte ich noch laden, das neueste Update leider nicht. Ich kaufe nicht oft Apps, aber hier lohnt sich die Pro Variante in jedem Fall. Weiter so liebes Entwicklerteam: Eine kleine Optimierung wäre noch schön: Beim Download von Musiktiteln auf das Handy bricht der Download leider sofort ab, wenn das Display dunkel wird. Wäre schön wenn der Download auch im Hintergrund weiterlaufen würden. Danke

author: Nhu Lai D.
title: Beste Musikplayer App die ich kenne
content:
Mir gefällt besonders an dieser App, dass die Entwickler sich so viel Mühe geben und regelmäßig viele tolle Verbesserungen bringen. Das findet man selten.

Außerdem ist die App sehr übersichtlich bietet tolle Metafunktionen. Man kann sehr leicht Bilder zu den eigenen Songs hinzufügen und Playlisten strukturiert und einfach ordnen.

Das klingt jetzt so banal, aber viele Entwickler bekommen das in ihrer App einfach nicht hin. Hier ist das einfach magisch gelungen.

Besonders gefällt mir, dass man auf dem iPhone sogar ein Hintergrund Musikalbum Cover hat, wenn man das iPhone ausschaltet. Dann hat man mir auf einem Radioplayer immer das Titelbild von dem Song klasse🥳✨

Ich kann jedem diese App nur wärmstens empfehlen.
Wenn ich könnte, würde ich sieben Sterne geben ✨

Für jeden echten  Musikliebhaber ein Muss😌🫶🎶💿

author: Signal2Noise
title: Useful
content:
I like this app for music playback of my local library. I would go Premium but the math is strange.

Monthly 0.99 = 11.88 / year
Yearly = 12.99 (so 1.11 more than paying by month)
Lifetime @ 39.99 is a 3 year cost up front so not sure how long FlacBox will be supported in the future. Lots of similar music apps seem to die out sooner than later.

author: Arlekin64
title: Trop long, beaucoup trop long !!!!
content:
Beaucoup trop long sur un iPad, iPhone ou même sur Mac pour récupérer toutes les métadonnées afin d’avoir tous les artistes, les albums et les morceaux.
Et quand je dis trop long, je parle de plusieurs heures ! Et sans que l’indexation ne soit terminée.
Quand vous voulez écouter un album, vous vous apercevez qu’il ne contient pas tous les morceaux ! La faute à cette indexation qui « travaille » au rythme d’un escargot unijambiste !
De plus, aucune synchronisation entre les différents appareils. Les playlists ne sont absolument pas synchronisées. 🤨


Dommage, l’application me plaisait bien. :(

De plus, il est signifié que quand on achète l’application ou quand on s’abonne, l’application peut être utilisée sur tous les appareils connectés au compte iTunes, ce qui est faux, pour le Mac il faut repasser à la caisse !

author: MaeLitroX
title: Good, but a few things
content:
Overall the app is really nice. Most apps don’t have custom speed control (especially without the pitch correction) so seeing it here was really nice. However, this app does lack things like Gapless Playback, and will instead skip the last second of a song or wait a bit, depending on the codec you’re using. Music controls also have a bit of delay, whether it be you’re skipping or replaying the song.

I also feel like the lifetime price is a tad bit overpriced, but that’s just my personal opinion. I do like the fact that ads don’t stop your music so I may just be prying too much lol.

Now my last thing, which is a personal preference: No playback count. I personally like seeing what songs/artists I’ve listened to much, so seeing how this wasn’t a thing that some other apps had made me a bit disappointed.

I’ll stick with it until I see something better though, much love <3

author: Pogodian
title: Study All Settings
content: This is a great app, but maybe not for people who want a fully automatic and transparent program. Take the time to carefully adjust your settings and you will be rewarded. However, adjusting the settings is very easy and totally comprehensible to anyone who understands basic file management. Highly recommended app for all but perhaps the computer illiterate.

author: Pampered Customer
title: Super f. externe Anbindung v. Speichern großer Musiksammlungen
content:
Nach ca. einem Jahr der Nutzung auf mehreren iPhones und vielen mit dem sehr engagierten Support beseitigten Fehlern, ist FLACBOX für mich die Referenz geworden, was unter iOS die Flexibilität extern angebundener Speichermedien für große Musiksammlungen (5-stellige Titelanzahl) angeht, beispielsweise auf an einer Festplatte, die an Fritz!Boxen angeschlossen ist. Der Zugriff funktioniert auch über VPN, ersetzt so externe Streamingdienstleister, zumindest, was meinen Musikbestand angeht. Sehr viele kleine hilfreiche Einzelfeatures erleichtern die Verwaltung. In der Bedienbarkeit und Ergonomie ist noch Luft nach oben. Die App wird auch gut gepflegt.
Freue mich über und auf weitere Verbesserungen. Diese Rezension ist ein Update.

author: badprogger
title: Perfect for Hi-Res Audio and Cloud Streaming!
content: Flacbox is an absolute must-have for anyone serious about their music. The app supports all the high-res audio formats I use, like FLAC and ALAC, and it plays them perfectly. It’s so convenient to stream directly from my cloud storage—Google Drive, Dropbox, and even my NAS are all supported. This means I can access my entire high-quality music collection anytime, anywhere, without cluttering up my phone's storage. The offline mode is another lifesaver for when I’m traveling. I can download the tracks I want and listen without needing internet access. I also love the playlist management. It’s super easy to create and organize playlists, and the ability to set audio bookmarks is a great touch. It helps me keep track of specific parts in longer mixes or podcasts. One of the best things about Flacbox is that it can open almost any audio file format. No matter what file I throw at it, the app plays it smoothly without any hassle. If you care about high-quality audio and need a flexible player that works with cloud services, Flacbox is unbeatable!

author: Airplanemode 2024
title: Great app with fundamental issues.
content:
This app is very good, but there are some fundamental problems that keep me from using it as my main music player. I am using iPhone 15 with iOS 18.1. 

1. The EQ. It lacks the ability to adjust the frequency and the width of each band. My headphones have a boost at ~3000hz, but I cannot cut that specific frequency and it keeps resonating. 
2. The seek bar in the app is broken. It never sets the right playback position. I go to 1:30, but I hear what's actually at 1:40 in that audio. To add insult to injury, these wrong positions aren't even the same time after time. It could be 1:40, or 1:42, or 1:38 - they're different each time. All of what I just described happened to me while listening to FLACs, so I don't know what the situation is with other formats.
3. The system media player widget behaviour is screwed as well with this player (this and also evermusic). Tap "next song" or "previous song" and boom. The seek bar is either at the end of the song, or just displays inaccurate information. Sometimes the numbers on the widget display real time of the song, but the seek bar doesn't move. To be completely fair, this is not the only music player that's been spotted doing this - Spotify seems to have completely forgotten to update their app for iOS 18 either. 

I hope the developers hear my feedback and it helps them make this app better, because the idea of the app is fantastic and I'm really to pay for it, ditching streaming services for good.

title: 機能充実

appleの社訓である「かゆいところに手を届けないけど他社の孫の手は使わせないぜ」が十分すぎるくらいに生かされてるapple musicやiTunes。かゆいところだらけのこれらを補完できる素晴らしいプレイヤーアプリです。
普通に使うのに必要な機能はほとんどあると言ってもいいくらい。アプリ起動即再生がないのは残念っちゃあ残念だけど、思いつく欠点はそれくらいしかない。
なにより機能の割に軽いので使いやすい。クラウド使っても山の中とかいかない限りはほとんど途切れない(某楽〇天モバイルを除く)。

title: Very good - but…
content:
Very nice tool, compliment!

What’s not that good: Explaining offline (in my case with OneDrive). I first made my all of my music (140 GB) available offline directly in the OneDrive app, plus I had a connection from the Flacbox app to OneDrive. Result: About 280GB of data, but the offline music was not available in Flacbox. Solution was to mark the music folder available for offline use IN the Flacbox up, connecting to OneDrive. Now, I only have it offline, which I wanted.

A bit more detailed manual would have helped.

And furthermore: There is no support at all. I sent two messages via contact form and never got an answer.

author: IDontNeedNoStupidNickname
title: So much better than native Apple app
content:
I really like Flacbox on my iPhone. With all my music stored in the cloud (in my case OneDrive), Flacbox basically gives me my own, personalized streaming service. No need to use iTunes to move a few songs here or there. No Apple tricks (like substituting the version of a song in THEIR library for the one I’m trying to download, then telling me I don’t have the rights to play it). Flacbox has excellent sound quality AND a full-featured equalizer accessible from within the app (no more switching out to Settings and selecting from an inane list of presets to compensate for the Apple player’s crappy sound).  And Flacbox, unlike Apple, isn’t constantly trying to SELL me something. 

There is a learning curve, but it’s worth it. Are there a few glitches? Sure. But the developer seems committed to improvements and fixes. They’re a small player competing on a field of giants. Respect!

author: UncleDuck9
title: Amazing hi rez streaming music app
content: Very impressive app that streams your music collection from a NAS or cloud service to your Apple devices. Lots of detailed settings in this app, making it a mature product for seasoned audiophiles. You can even change settings for use with your external DAC, and the addition of an equalizer makes my Boomer heart happy. Built in tags editor is also much appreciated. Delighted with my purchase. Personal wish list to the developers: add a PDF viewer to the file manager or charge an upgrade fee for PDF and ebook viewing. Then I don’t need to leave the app to read something, or juggle with split screens on my device. Not a complaint, however, because this app is already a top performer. Thank you to the developers.

author: Alex B B
title: Excellent app to replace Spotify
content:
I must be old school, in that I prefer the simplicity of having easy access to my music collection, stored on cloud, and I do not love being bombarded with suggestions for new music.
As such, competing music services, such as YouTube, premium, or Spotify do not work for me.
This app is one stop shop for everything music and audiobook related: store all of my files on my own cloud(not forced to use iCloud), cache whatever I wanna listen to for the next several weeks for off-line use. As of the time of writing, no other iOS app can do this.
Lastly, the developers are active constantly making improvements , fixing bugs and API interfaces with many cloud services. This is a lot of work, and the developer should be applauded for keeping it up!

author: HNX KNNX
title: Wahnsinn! Endlich ein mp3/m4a/flac Player
content:
Ich hab gefühlt 100 Player getestet! Das Teil kann mit mp3s etc. aus anderen apps befüllt werden, man kann auch via WLAN oder iOS-Dateien importieren. und und und ! 
Dateien können beliebig versendet werden, mp3-tags können automatisch oder manuell geändert werden. Cover werden automatisch geladen. CarPlay funktioniert bestens.
Equalizer kann verschiedene Profile speichern. 
Selbst ein automatisches synchronisieren mit beliebigen online speichern ist möglich. (was ich allerdings nicht nutze). 
Danke! Der Preis ist mehr als gerechtfertigt!

content: 自從之前的更新之後，我iOS 13.3的iPhone就一直打不開程式，每次都會閃退。不論是重新下載，甚至是iPhone重置後依然存在這個問題，本來想說之後的更新應該就會修正了，但這個問題似乎沒有被發現，如果製作團隊有看到，再麻煩你們修正了，謝謝！這個軟體的排版，以及中文化的介面老實說是我至目前為止遇過數一數二厲害的，而且在歌曲的聆聽與編輯上面都十分順手，所以我覺得他值得五顆星。

author: gimmyua
title: Відтворення пісень
content: В разі коли заходиш в розкладку пісні в музичній бібліотеці і запускаєш грати першу пісню, то після виходу з бібліотеки в сам плеєр в чергу відтворення додається лише 100 пісень. В разі коли робиш це все через кнопку грати в розкладках пісні в музичній бібліотеці, то додаються вже всі пісні. Це дуже не зручно, адже іноді хочеться запустити програвання з якоїсь конкретної пісні, а далі щоб грала вся музична бібліотека. Версія повна.

author: That1HoneyBadger
title: Almost perfect!
content:
This is a necessity for anyone who gets their music from other places other than streaming services.  A lot of great artists don't have everything up on streaming services -- deep cuts, rarities, etc.  So having a solid Audio Player is a must.  Flacbox is the best Ive come across, and its at a reasonable price point, unlike the other very excellent (but deeply overpriced subscription based Audio Player).

One thing I would like to see, is a bit of polish in the UI.  Its good, but its not great.  I find that the navigation makes for a less than smooth UX.  Being able to have Music, Artist, Album, Song on the bottom level would be nice, or maybe being able to customize the bottom level would be nice.  Also, I think a bit more elegance in the interface is in order, something akin to the Sony Walkman (Or maybe even implementing skins)!

Otherwise, fantastic app -- a permanent app of my iPhone!  Keep it up!

author: Vindictor
title: Just missing SSH
content:
Great music player. It can play Flac, and many other formats, from your Google Drive, OneDrive, and various other cloud storage providers. We just had another update, and I got excited for a moment…. Because the only feature I’m REALLY waiting for is the ability to connect to a music library via SSH / SFTP. Then my entire home music library will be available on my iPhone and iPad, no matter where I am, without having to first upload it anywhere, or pay for extra cloud storage that I don’t need as I have plenty of storage at home, on the end of a fibre connection. Having said that, I do also wish the music player would display the codec of the current song, it would make it easier if you have both AAC and Flac copies of albums in a library. Would also be cool if it would show a “24 bit” or “HD Audio” icon when playing back hires files. of course this should be toggleable for those who don’t want to see this info, but I’d leave it on all the time. Small niggles. The player looks great, works great… sounds great! This app has the only software EQ that I’ve ever thought actually enhances the sound. Hoping the next update will provide SSH (I know a number of people who’re waiting for this) and display FLAC or AAC or MP3 on screen during playback. Thanks for allowing me to enjoy my flac collection without having to convert everything…. At least while I’m at home (for now) via SMB. I’ll be dancing in the streets when I can stream my music, outside, via SSH.

EDIT: I was just looking at installing Nextcloud on my server. Sadly I don’t see this in the list of supported cloud servers, either? It’s just that having TBs of storage at home, it’s a waste to pay for extra cloud services from which to stream music.  So I’m still hoping SSH (or maybe next cloud) can be supported, soon.  Hell, if they could implement SSH/SFTP file browsing/streaming then they could even branch out and allow remote photo viewing, and make a full remote media viewer / player.

author: jaa2023
title: Tried again and love it!
content:
A little less than a year ago I started trying to stream my own (mostly high rez) music collection.  I tried several of the obvious choices in the App Store, including Flacbox.  It was all pretty frustrating and I gave up on it.  Too much effort for buggy software, poor synchronization, missing songs, etc. 

Next I tried Roon and Roon Arc.  That showed promise at first - but had much of the same problems only at a very high price point.  I gave up on that too (I use an Aurender in my main (old fashioned 2 big speaker) system.  

A week or two ago I decided to try a few App Store solutions again to see if they might have become more viable.  Flacbox was the clear winner and I’ve been working with it on both my iPhone and my iPad.  I am thrilled with Flacbox v4.2!  Look, nothing in this space is perfect yet.  You need some patience and some time to re-download, re-sync, change song orders (with a great sort feature), etc.  But now I have 125gb of uncompressed music on both my Apple devices and can use the online files (synced to my Google Drive) to replace downloads whenever I want (I haven’t streamed my online files much yet).  Lastly, the Flacbox album display is beautiful. 

 I highly recommend Flacbox and now just hope the app developer has a sustainable business model and keeps this app and future versions running like v4.2 does now.

author: ktraxx89
title: A BEAUTIFUL workaround for listening to your own creations
content:
In a world of endless subscriptions/cloud services and DRM music streaming apps, somehow the simple functions of a personal MP3 player don’t seem possible anymore - until I found this app…

I love to mix my own beats as a hobby. To listen to the mixes on the go after all that hard work (best part) I used to use Apple Music to upload my music to my iCloud library (don’t ask me how) but for whatever reason this little trick stopped working on me. That’s when I discovered this little gem. So simple to use. Just create a DropBox account, plop in the ridiculously massive WAV’s, FLAC’s, AIF’s or whatever you fancy, open this app and bang - instantly available on the go. LOVVE how you can download to your phone with this too. No more blitzing through my data listening to massive 500+ mB WAV files on repeat. Massive functionality for a free app. Thank you for putting this out there 🙏🙏🙏

author: Mohawke
title: Best music player
content:
As a Linux user this is really the only way to use my music collection with my iPhone. I set up SMB and pull songs down and build the playlists in Flacbox. Been doing that for years but it was recently broke.

In a world of monthly fees just to listen to music with a ton of overly feature packed garbage players, also asking for monthly fees and your email address, so you can listen offline without draining your cell plans usage or simply grabbing music from the Apple player that works the same way this was awesome.

The only issue? Lately when I add songs via SMB, even after waiting for them to download, some are missing and no amount of attempts to get them downloaded works. Fix that and I’ll give it five stars. The http process works fine but it’s tedious. I really hope this player sticks around or I’ll have write my own and use TestFlight, because I hate customers and don’t want to pay dev fees and own other Apple garbage products to produce one piece of software.

author: wanderm

I don’t have a problem with Apple Music / iTunes, and I certainly don’t resent my iDevice, but as an old-timer I really appreciate this “old-school” substitute.

author: andwalkinglightly369
title: 5 out of 5
content:
Excellent app interface and option. Great additional features that I hadn’t tried on other apps as speed control, in depth eq, wifi sharing, etc.
Works great, even when using the limited version with ads you get an awesome app, a good option if you like different media players. 

Been using it for 3 years.

You can share music with wifi connection and download files to any computer. 

It really is a Swiss Army knife of media players.

The premium version is really affordable, too.

The only downside I’ve found is that on limited version you get less than 10 playlists available.

On limited version you can avoid ads by going on airplane mode, anyway, great app, works great and provides a better more customizable option to other media players.👏✨

title: For those missing control of their music
content: I’m in the UK and to buy the full app (one time purchase not subscription !!!!) is less than a tenner. If you spend that the app does most you can think of and more. I haven’t used it much but today someone sent me an mp3 and I was glad to have a place to save the mp3 (in files>>Flacbox). I hope to sort out my library soon :)

Dann gibts nur eine unübersichtliche Listenansicht. Oder ich lasse die Alben anzeigen, dann ist Rock, Klassik, Soundtracks etc. wild durcheinander. Nachdem ich meine Klassik und meine Rock-Sammlung streng getrennt haben möchte, kann ich dadurch FlacBox momentan nicht gebrauchen. Schade, denn ansonsten ist die App fantastisch! Also: Albenansicht unter Genres implementieren, dann gibts gerne den fünften Stern!

author: Acorpze
title: Decent but needs improvement
content:
Good app so far, I’ve been using it to play music in OGG Vorbis format.

I would give it more stars but here is why I didn’t:
-There seems to be a major stuttering issue,
the app works fine alone but will start stuttering once you tab out to another app and sometimes even crashes.

-I think sound normalization is a must have since not all audio is recorded/produced with the same output volume, often resulting in noticeable differences in loudness.

-Not a major issue but.. pitch correction feels a bit off.
I feel like pitch correction just attempts to shift the pitch without properly resampling/stretching the audio and sounds faulty.

In most other audio players I am able to change the pitch of the audio and it will sound like as if the audio was actually recorded with said pitch which is not the case here unfortunately.

I don’t have much else to add since I have all my files locally downloaded so I cannot really review the cloud features of this app.

author: Gogobop
title: FlacBox
content: This app is great for iPhone users if they have a laptop/PC and lots of music to transfer using the WI-FI transfer option as so far it’s been flawless. The equaliser can accommodate many custom settings that are quick to access for different headphones and/or genre’s of music. There is plenty of settings and options in general that make this app shine brighter than anything else I have tried over the years for iPhone and I couldn’t be happier finding it. One thing that I would like though to make it perfect for me is to be able to have a loudness level setting to level out all songs to a similar ( preferably same ) volume to save having to raise and lover the volume. This is an issue because of the loudness wars plus different sources of master volume settings across all music and not the fault of FlacBox, it would just be great to have a way of getting around this issue that drives many people crazy. I purchased this app because I want to support the developers and would be happy to purchase more extra features that would take this app to the far and beyond. Thank you FLacBox Creators ⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️

author: thefourthcolour
title: Two issues
content: I have been using this app for my music projects recently, and I love it and think it is much superior to other similar programs, but there are two pretty big issues that are getting me. One: I have a lot of songs that "segue", or bleed into each other and are directly connected, and this app takes a few seconds to apparently "load" the next song. On Apple Music and other programs, seamless segues are allowed, but on Flacbox, there is a pause, which screws up the listening experience. Two: If I am inactive on the app for a day or so, my entire music library erases, and I have to reupload everything from my cloud drive. As soon as it is there, it reattaches the audio tags and assigns songs to their artists and albums, but it takes at least 15 minutes. Also, when I try to use the app on my phone, under the same account, I have to upload my entire music library, again, and wait for all the albums to be assigned. I haven't figured out a way to stop this so far. Both issues are really annoying, but otherwise the app works well. Please pay attention to this review, as I have reviewed the app multiple times stating the same issues, and I can't find a way to email/contact the creator. Thanks

author: Super Cheese
title: Everything you would want in a player
content: I use this app to listen to podcasts and sermons that I want to have locally but not in my iTunes library. Great for managing files as it has robust cloud service interface.  Since I study the material bookmark feature is awesome and works well with apple watch so you can bookmark while walking and listening.  My only gripe is that I wish the developer would fix the lock screen controls to rewind and bookmark. As opposed to bookmark and fast forward. Would be five star if this change was made. It is a little annoying that is change or improvement has not been implemented especially because I have been asking for it for over half a year, and the developer takes pride in consistently updating the app. Please implement this change.

author: Magpie1969
title: Riproduzione non gapless in streaming DLNA
content:
Utilizzo questa app esclusivamente per riprodurre i file audio da server casalingo via DLNA, in particolare file ad alta risoluzione (riprodotti attraverso un DAC).
La riproduzione è OK, però via DLNA non è gapless. Questo è un grosso limite che altre app analoghe non hanno, ed è parecchio fastidioso se si ascoltano album live o concept album senza interruzioni fra i brani.
Ho provato ad aumentare la cache ma non ha funzionato.
Spero che in una nuova versione questo problema venga risolto.

author: HawaiiMaui
title: EQ works on iPad, SMB2 Win10 sharing
content:
I can connect to file shares on my Win10 PC. This is amazing, since I WFH I can use my iPad to play MP3's or FLAC and not need to download files and use up my precious (paltry) 32GB of storage. [Any other app that claims SMB but hasn't been updated since 2018 won't let you authenticate with Windows. MS disabled SMB1 and the devs needed to update to SMB2 or SMB3; this app has kept up to date.]

This app also has an EQ, this basically lets me use any halfway decent earphones and make them sound much better. Buying this app with EQ was much cheaper than buying high end wireless earphones. I've used EQ's for years with speakers to neutralize the room acoustics and love them with headphones too. The EQ sliders were missing on iPad but a few emails with the dev and they've got it working in the Dec 2020 build.

The SMB (cloud) playback also seems less buggy, or my Wi-Fi is behaving. The app was pretty unforgiving of bad network connections, but this one hasn't crashed for hours. (Their MP3 EQ app has 3x retry options in the settings but the FLAC one doesn't.)

Müzik arada donuyor. Başka bir uygulamaya geçerken ya da herhangi bir eylemde şarkılar donuyor. Arada da tamamen duruyor uygulamaya girip düzeltmek gerekiyor. Umarım bu sorunlar kısa zamanda düzelir.

Ek: Arkadaşlar birkaç saat kullanınca sinir krizi geçirdim. Şurda biraz kaliteli müzik dinleyelim dedik müzikten soğuduk. En ufak bir aktivitede müzik devam ediyor gözüküyor ama ses yok, ne durdurabiliyorum ne şarkıyı geçebiliyorum. Anca uygulamayı kapatıp girince düzeliyor, ayrıca optimizasyon sorunları var. author: Vafim7684
title: Отзыв
content: Хороший поейер, но косяки, что появились после предпоследнего обновления надо починить. Стал сжирать песни. Запускаешь плейлист играет все по очереди, но может съесть две минуты начала песни, или вообще кинуть на конец. Причем ни в одном и том же месте, а в разных. Далее уши... Вынимаешь наушник, проигрывание останавливается, потом может восстановится или нет. Иногда начинает играть, играет одну песню и дальше стоп. Причем даже на экране блокировки ничего нет. Открываешь приложение, видно что играет, но звука нет, ни в динамике ни в ушах. Выбираешь другой трек ничего, закрываешь приложение открываешь снова все работает. Грешил на уши, на обновление ios но на других плейерах такого нет.

author: LudoRizza
title: Bug fixes and suggestions
content:
First of all. I want to say that the app is awesome. I use it everyday. I want them to fix a bug that crashes the player when I keep the music paused for long periods with the screen turned off and the wireless headphones still connected. Sometimes it happens when i switch from a music to another. I don’t really now when and why it happens.  
Also, I would like to have the possibility to “link” a folder to a playlist, so, every time I put an mp3 file into the folder, it is automatically added to the playlist. 
Finally, every time I upload a song, or when I listen to a playlist, after a while, the songs are not sorted as I wanted. They start to move from a place to another. It would be great if it is, also, possible not to show the music covers. I know I can de-activate them from the settings, but the app still downloads them
Thanks for your work

author: TARA〜
title: 頻繁に落ちる→アプリ削除＆再DLで改善？
content:
ipadでクラウドやNASから直接聞けて、かつイコライザつきなので大変重宝させていただいてます！(wmaも聞けるので助かってます！)

有料版にアップグレードしてから頻繁にアプリが落ちるようになって原因もわからずやきもきしてましたが、同系アプリEvermusicも有償版で同症状が出たため、ダメもとでいったん端末からアプリを削除したのち再ダウンロード、「設定」＞「購入の復元」をしてから再生。

これで自分の環境下では今のところ安定して再生できています。皆さんの症状ごとに原因は違ってると思いますが、もし困ってる人の改善の一助になれば。改善例の一つとしてご参考までに。

もしまた不具合など出てきたら報告させていただきます。

title: Music stops inside a playlist
content: Hi. Just wanted to inform you that the music is not playing inside the playlist. The playlist automatically stops even in you are in a continuous play. By the way I am using the pro version because i think pro will solve the problem but it didnt. Justo to give you a history, I started using free evermusic, then purchased inapp pro version and did not fixed the problem. I then uninstalled it and downloaded the pro evermusic version itself but again didnt resolved the issue. Upon browsing and searching the right app, I find Flacbox. It has the same interface of evermusic, which I didnt know if they are if the same creator and the 6s app did the thing of continous play (my 11pro app has the same problem). But now after the update, same problem now arisen in my 6s. I mean I actually paid for the same three apps just to get the best experience. I hope you resolve the issue because I really like your app. Thanks, more power!

author: Gurgge
title: New Version introduced problems
content:
The team behind FlacBox are doing a great job, and I am using this app a lot to listen to music collaborators send me, longer podcasts I am taking with me, etc. It’s ability to open and play music from emails and organize it into folders and playlists directly on the phone is one of the best on iOS.

Unfortunately the new version removed the “Continue Playback” function on imported mp3 files. Pressing play to resume listening long files (2-3 hours, 170mb) doesn’t work to resume playback. It never did. But there was a “Continue Playback” function in the context menu which did just that. Unfortunately it is now gone, and so this doesn’t work anymore.... and the ability to listen to long tracks is one of the most important functions for me. Hope they can fix that.

북마크된 목록을 눌러도 거리로 바로 이동되지 않고 렉이 걸리는 일이 종종 생기는데 북마크 된 곳으로 빠르게
이동할 수 있게 수정해주셨으면 좋겠습니다.
그리고 북마크 메모 내용 전체를 한눈에 쭉 볼 수 있게
전체 보기 식으로도 만들어주셨으면 합니다. 지금은 각 파일을 하나를 지정해서 눌러야 해당 파일의 북마크가 뭔지 보이는 형태라 한눈에 북마크 메모 내용이 쭉 보이지 않아 불편합니다. 재생목록 하나당 메모된 북마크 내용이 전부 보이도록 개선해주시면 더 좋을듯 합니다.

author: jfontana
title: El mejor reproductor para audio de calidad en iOS
content:
Si te importa la calidad del sonido cuando escuchas música, éste es tu reproductor para iOS.

Llevaba mucho tiempo buscando la manera de disfrutar de mi colección de álbumes en formato digital (FLAC, DSD, DFF) de alta definición (24/96, 24/192) desde mi dispositivo (usando un DAC y buenos auriculares) y al final lo he encontrado.

La relación calidad precio de la versión PRO es insuperable. No sólo reproduce formatos de alta definición con un sonido excelente sino que te permite conectarte a tu ordenador y a otros dispositivos de tu red local a través de la WiFi. Toda mi música a mi alcance desde cualquier lugar de la casa.

Me gustaría que hubiera algo parecido para el Mac. Allí tienes apps como Pine pero Flacbox es realmente muy superior. Los reproductores de calidad decentes (e.g. Audirvana) tienen precios muy altos.

Gracias desarrolladores de Flacbox por crear una app tan fantástica. Desarrollarla también para MacOS, por favor!

author: Robthefluter
title: Some issues
content: Most of my ripped CD collection is from windows media player and is in WMA format. Latterly I did some in Flac. This and VLC are the only players I have found other than Windows Groove which will handle both formats as well as work directly from my OneDrive cloud store. Groove is no longer available for iOS and I want a replacement. The scan speed and artwork issues noted by other reviews are bad but not necessarily showstoppers. I have also noticed that the sound quality from WMA files is inferior to that produced by the VLC player on the same files. With Flac files there is less difference so I think there is a faulty WMA codec in use here. At least this app will attempt to put albums together from the cloud files. VLC Doesn’t do that and seems unacceptably basic in operation.

title: Mutlaka Denemelisiniz
content:
Androidden IOSa geçtiğimden beri müzik koleksiyonumu dinleme konusunda kıvranıyordum. Popüler programlar dahil istediğim gibi bir şey bulamamıştım. Ta ki bu programı bulana kadar. Senkronizasyon yöneticisi işini çok iyi yapıyor. Bilgisayardan wi fi ile parçaları indirmek düşündüğümden de güzel. Daha bir çok kolaylık var. 
Eksikliğini hissettiğim yerler: Hi-Res parçalar dinlerken bunun bir sembolle köşede belirtilmesi, yine çalarken bit ve kbps bilgilerini görmek isterim. Aynı şekilde 24bit parça çalıyorum ama sen kulaklığıma aynı şekilde bunu yolluyormusun yoksa kırpıyormusun görmek isterim. Yani parçanın giriş ve çıkış bit ve kbps bilgilerini çalarken görmek isterim. 
Güncelleme bekliyoruz.

title: Please bring back "Continue Last Play" option
content:
30/01/2020
The feature by which I was able to continue playing the last song at the exact moment it was playing, has been removed in latest updates. It was quite beneficial to many users I'm sure. @Dev please bring it back.

== Full screen ads - FIXED! ==
The app is one of the best free audio player apps in the iOS store, but the full screen ads seriously suck. There's no way to close them, I have to wait for a specific time after which they close automatically. You should provide an explicit close button to close the ads. Provide a timer for the closing time.

author: Porrohman_
title: Frustrating but promising?
content:
Shows much promise but fails drastically for me as using as an SMB front end. I’ve only found this and Music Streamer that are in the area I want, both have inadequacies.

1. Scan time is just out of this world ridiculous, it’s like it uses carrier pigeons. MS takes about 20 minutes to scan the folder that this presumably would take several days, I haven’t had the patience to find out yet.

2. My Flac/MP3 collection includes all of the artwork in the folders or embedded. This app ignores that and goes to the internet to find the wrong images 30-40% of the time. There is no way of updating the image yourself so you are supposed to just put up with an ambient album that has a picture of a rap artist... MS deals with this almost perfectly, using the folder images and has the ability to copy/paste an image when needed.

3. It doesn’t seem to pick up the album/artist tags correctly all of the time, but maybe it’s because I haven’t had a scan complete.

Outside of these major problems, I really like the look of it and the play behaviour which certainly wins over MS which also has it’s own bizarre usability choices. I will keep an eye on this in the hope it will be improved, but in the meantime MS wins my loyalty. Wrong artwork is far far far worse than none at all...

author: Meon123
title: Versatile Flacbox
content: Have been using Flacbox for about 2 months and it seems stable for my requirements that includes Hi Res.  Sound quality is good on ALAC and FLAC files.  I also use the Onkyo Hi Fi player and Qobuz for streaming and purchasing music.  Flacbox has very useful features including easily importing files from cloud storage and a graphic equaliser that works on the digital source to my Chord Mojo; Flacbox is the only App that can do this. I like to slightly adjust the sound frequencies to suit my tastes and hearing.  Ive compared sound quality with the Onkyo and I think there is slight clipping of high frequency clarity with Flacbox (equaliser off) but the ability to adjust the bass on Flacbox is brilliant.

author: Silly/Hopes
title: Лучшее, что есть, но с некоторыми недостатками
content:
Здравствуйте. Хочу поделиться своим мнением по поводу Flacbox. 
Единственная возможность слушать скачанную музыку с айфона для меня - это приложение. У меня куплена премиум версия.
Недостатки, конечно, есть:
1) Медлительность.
2) Временами песня не играет. Приходится пару раз нажать на кнопку воспроизведения.
3) Хочешь слушать скачанный плейлист? Будь готов скачать уже скачанные песни заново. Вот это мне не нравится больше всего. Сами знаете, что на айфонах память ограничена. У меня версия на 32гб. Хочу слушать определённын плейлисты, но не желаю скачивать музыку, которая и так скачана. Зачем эти сложности?
4) Часто замечаю, что при скачивании из Google Drive нескольких файлов, внизу не появляется маленькое красное "облачко" с количеством скачиваемых песен. Помогают танцы с бубном. И да, естественно, при отсутствии этого облачка сами песни также не скачиваются.
Но я всё равно рекомендую это приложение и продолжу им пользоваться. Всем хорошего дня!

content: 유료 구매하여 만족스럽게 사용하고 있습니다. 현제 아이폰으로 가장 최신인 ios 13.1 베타버전을 사용중입니다. ios 12를 사용중인 아이패드에서 재생목록을 만드는 것은 오류가 없지만 현제 13 베타버전을 사용중인 아이폰에서는 재생목록 이름생성창에서 확인 취소 버튼이 뜨지 않아 만들지도 못하고 뒤로 가지도 못하는 상황이 발생했습니다. 아직 정식버전이 아니라 그럴수록 있다고 생각하나 다른 입들은 사용하며 문제가 된적은 없었습니다. ios 13과 관련하여 버그를 해결해주셨으면 합니다. 부탁드립니다.

author: audiofeel.flac
title: Дорогие разработчики
content: Сделайте пожалуйста режим двойных функции кнопок громкости(пусть такой режим можно будет подключить в настройках): один раз нажимаешь vol+ увеличивается громкость, два раза(быстрое нажатие)- переключается песня вперёд с vol- соответсвенно, и позвольте нам решать период времени который будет распознаваться как «быстрое нажатие». Это удобно людям которые не имеют микрофона на наушниках или не имеют кнопок на микрофоне( профессиональные наушники с несколькими драйверами не всегда предполагают наличие кнопок. Ещё хотелось бы более подробное описание треков с указанием их битрейта, частоты и др. А так спасибо вам за приложение, виду что стараетесь, недавно вот мне на радость эквалайзер подучил возможность сохранять пресеты.Приложение хорошее всем советую.

title: Tolle App mit einer Schwäche

Downloaden möchte ich die Lieder von meinem Server nicht sondern lediglich abspielen, das klappt zwar sehr gut aber mir fehlen einfach die Cover. Sobald man diese von der SMB Freigabe downloaded ist das Cover sofort da (ist in der Datei auch entsprechend eingebettet). Bitte diesen kleinen Fehler nich beheben dann bin ich überglücklich und kann vor allem bei CarPlay ohne erst während der Fahrt lesen zu müssen entsprechend schnell durchschalten.

author: Godbluffer
title: Gapless not working anymore etc
content:
To my shock I found out that gapless playback is not working anymore with Apple lossless (.m4a) files, and something worse had happened with FLAC files; only ONE of the two stereo channels was being played, as ‘mono’ through both speakers.

That second issue has now been resolved, but gapless playback *must* make a return in the next update.

author: Kinder-Memory-Test
title: Bester Player im App Store
content: Ich habe lange nach einer Alternative zur Apple-eigenen Musik App gesucht, welche gefühlt über die Jahre immer weiter verschlimmbessert und mit dem von mir nicht gewollten Apple Music Abo Dienst überfrachtet wurde. Hier lassen sich meine mp3 Sammlung komfortabel via WebDAV wireless übertragen, meine eigenen Playlisten erstellen und pflegen (die dann auch nicht ungewollt beim Sync in iTunes verändert oder dupliziert werden) und das Zusammenspiel mit Apple Watch und Co. funktioniert auch einwandfrei. Die App ist jeden Cent wert und jederzeit würde ich auch zukünftig einen Inn-App Obulus für die Entwickler zahlen - weiter so!). Apps dieser Qualität sind leider selten geworden in der heutigen Zeit. Absolut empfehlenswert.

author: moon_the
title: Excellent player, but with a room for improvement
content:
Nice interface, folder playback support (such a shame that only a few players can do this! ), customizable. My favorite player so far. 

The only reason I'm not giving it a 5 star rating are a couple of annoyances: 
- not all .cue files can be parsed, even if in other players they are fine 
- buttons to skip forward/backward do not show in widget 
- swipe right action is implemented inconsistenly: f.e. it works in settings, but not while navigating music folders. Back button in top left corner is just bad ux for mere humans and not some aliens with extended fingers

author: shortofapicnic
title: I like it but...
content:
It still crashes pretty frequently. 
But the absolutely, bone-crushingly, life-sappingly worst thing about this app is when you want to add songs from your NAS to be stored on your phone. If you have a large library and want to add a song by, say, Yazoo or ZZ Top, you have to scroll through the hundreds of artistes who come before in the alphabetical list. There’s no quick way to jump to a particular letter as there is in iTunes. And then - this makes me want to cry with frustration - the app jumps back to the music list. You can’t stay within the Yazoo folder and choose songs from a second album - no - you have to go through the whole scrolling rigmarole again. Aaarghhhh! Sort it out, guys.

author: Bocheng Gu
title: Problematic SMB streaming/playback in public
content:
First off, this is a great app for streaming FLAC music from my PC or NAS *at home*. BUT when I try to use it outside in public places to stream from my wifi-direct card reader (i.e. Kingston MobileLite Wireless G3 or Apotop DW21), it's absolutely nightmare! Below is the problems I encountered with the app listed in order from the most severe to the least. The post is quite long because I try to be specific with the issues so that they may be easier for you to identify and hopefully fix in future updates. So bear with me.

1. The SMB connection only works in purely wifi environment. Whenever and wherever my device is connected to BOTH the wifi from my wireless card reader AND the 4G/3G mobile network, the app just wouldn't let me access my wireless card reader at all! Yet there's no such problem when I use other SMB streaming apps (like nPlayer) to stream from the same source. So I'm pretty sure it's the problem with this particular app, which brings me to my next issue.

2. The app can access the files on my wireless card reader ONLY when I cut off the mobile network and let my device connect to only my wifi card reader without the internet access (Specifically mobile network. Wifi bridge works fine though). With the above settings, the app works well at streaming FLAC from my wireless card reader *at home*. The playback is smooth, no glitches or pauses between songs whatsoever. HOWEVER, when I try to use it outside in public places with the same devices and same setup, everything changes! The app still can stream my FLAC files but it always takes about 30 seconds to load the next FLAC file, yet if the next song is in MP3 or AAC format it plays immediately. For an app that specializes in streaming FLAC music, this is totally unacceptable! And I know this is an inherent issue with the app because *yet again* I encountered no such playback problem with other SMB streaming apps. nPlayer for example streams my FLAC files just fine everywhere (at home or outside) and with or without connecting to mobile network. This app however only seems to work smoothly in SMB mode at home in purely wifi environment, which renders it pretty much pointless since streaming FLAC music outside is my main goal for this app.

3. This is pretty minor compared to the first two main issues. But  for some reason the app wouldn't display the album art for some songs, which brings me to my next gripe.

4. I much prefer the old UI where during playback you see just the album art without the pause button in the center and no title bar in the bottom, both of which IMHO are completely redundant since the app already shows the title of the song on top and the control buttons at the bottom. Also the rotation animation of the album art is a bit annoying. Can we get rid of that too? Or at least have the ability to turn it off?

Finally, thank you for your patience and time. I'm looking forward to your reply and possible solutions for the issues I listed above.

My Setup:

Streaming devices: iPhone SE or iPad mini 4, both have been updated to the latest iOS version 10.3.1

Streaming sources: Apotop Wi-Copy (DW21) or Kingston MobileLite Wireless G3 (MLWG3), both use 2.4g wifi-direct to connect to my iPhone or iPad.
